location: Uruk, Mesopotamia (modern-day Iraq); Cedar Forest (Mount Lebanon); Mount Nimush; the 'end of the earth'
narrative_summary: Gilgamesh, the oppressive king of Uruk, is challenged by the wild man Enkidu, created by the gods. They become friends and embark on epic adventures, slaying the monster Humbaba and the Bull of Heaven. After Enkidu dies as punishment from the gods, Gilgamesh, terrified of his own mortality, undertakes a perilous quest to find Utanapishtim, the immortal survivor of the Great Flood, to learn the secret of eternal life. Despite his arduous journey and trials, he ultimately fails to achieve immortality and returns to Uruk, a changed man who now appreciates his city's enduring legacy.
